title: Hex.pm API Vocabulary
description: Domain terms and concepts used throughout the Hex.pm API.

terms.definition: A reusable library or application published to the Hex.pm registry. A package can have multiple releases.
A specific versioned artifact of a package. Each release contains a tarball, metadata, and dependency requirements.
A Hex repository hosting packages. The default public repository is hex.pm. Organizations can have private repositories.
A group account that can own private packages and manage members with different roles (admin, write, read).
A user who has publishing rights to a package. Packages can have multiple owners.
A token used for authenticating API requests. Keys have named permissions scoped to specific domains and resources.
A compressed archive (`.tar`) containing a package release. Must conform to the Hex tarball specification.
A status applied to a release indicating it should not be used. Reasons include: other, invalid, security, deprecated, renamed.
The documentation hosting service for Hex packages at hexdocs.pm. Documentation is published as a tarball.
The Erlang virtual machine (Bogdan/Björn's Erlang Abstract Machine). Hex supports all BEAM languages including Elixir, Erlang, and Gleam.
OAuth2 flow (RFC 8628) used for obtaining access tokens on devices that cannot easily display a browser. Used as the primary OAuth method in Hex.
Optional additional security requiring a TOTP code sent in the x-hex-otp header for write operations.
100 requests per minute per IP for unauthenticated requests; 500 per minute for authenticated users. Status returned in X-RateLimit-* headers.
HTTP caching mechanism using ETag and Last-Modified headers with If-None-Match and If-Modified-Since. Returns 304 Not Modified if unchanged.
A record of actions performed by users or systems, including actor, action type, timestamp, and parameters.
